parentQuestion: The organelle primarily responsible for cellular ATP production is the ___.

scaffolds.q: ATP is the cell's main source of ___.
scaffolds.explain: ATP (adenosine triphosphate) provides energy for cellular processes. When cells need energy, they break down ATP to release it. It's the universal energy currency — accepted everywhere in the cell.

scaffolds.q: Cellular respiration converts glucose into ___ that cells can use.
scaffolds.explain: Cellular respiration breaks down glucose (food) to produce ATP, which cells use as energy for their activities. This process primarily occurs in mitochondria — the cellular power plants. Don't lose this thread:.
